steps.title: Determine what "present the study and its findings" requires
steps.content:
The task is to present both the study (who, when, what was studied) and its findings (what Chinsamy-Turan concluded and why). A choice that omits the conclusion fails the "findings" requirement; a choice that omits the researcher or date fails the "study" requirement.
  • Choice A — 'In 2021, Chinsamy-Turan studied pterosaur jawbones and was initially unsure if the bones belonged to juveniles or adults.': Describes the researcher's initial uncertainty, not the conclusion — no findings presented. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Pterosaur jawbones located in the Sahara Desert were the focus of a 2021 study.': Mentions the study but omits the researcher's name and any findings. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In a 2021 study, Chinsamy-Turan used advanced microscope techniques to analyze the jawbones of pterosaurs, flying reptiles that existed millions of years ago.': Describes methodology only; never states what she concluded. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In a 2021 study, Chinsamy-Turan determined that pterosaur jawbones located in the Sahara Desert had few growth lines relative to the bones of fully grown pterosaurs and thus belonged to juveniles.': Names the researcher and date, states the evidence (few growth lines), and states the conclusion (belonged to juveniles). ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: Choice D is the only option that presents both the study (Chinsamy-Turan, 2021) and its findings (jawbones belonged to juveniles, supported by the growth-line evidence). That matches choice D — D is correct.
